title: Osteomeles pernettyoides
taxonomy: Osteomeles pernettyoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It is native to the Hawaiian Islands and is a member of the genus Osteomeles, which consists of six species of shrubs and trees. This plant is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 2 meters in height and has oval-shaped leaves. The classification of Osteomeles pernettyoides also places it within the order Rosales, a group of flowering plants that includes roses and other related species. Further classification groups it within the class Magnoliopsida, a subclass of flowering plants.

Its berries, leaves, and roots have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. intro: Osteomeles pernettyoides, also known as the Hawaiian haweo, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae that is endemic to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. The health benefits of Osteomeles pernettyoides include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as arthritis and high blood pressure.
